card.intuition: The Document Object Model (DOM) is a tree-like representation of your HTML that the browser creates so it can programmatically access and change the page structure, style, and content.
card.analogy: A physical building's blueprint: the HTML is the original paper drawing, and the DOM is the actual 3D model the architects use to see how moving a wall (element) affects the rest of the structure.
card.time_complexity: O(N) to build; O(1) to access by ID; O(N) to traverse
when_to_use: Understanding how JavaScript interacts with HTML
Optimizing performance by minimizing DOM manipulation
Debugging layout issues and element visibility
gotchas: The DOM is NOT the HTML source; it can be different if JavaScript has modified it or the browser fixed broken tags (like adding a missing </tbody>)
Frequent DOM updates are slow because they trigger the 'Render Pipeline' repeatedly

exercise.prompt: Identify the standard tree traversal method most commonly used by the browser to build the DOM from HTML tokens.
exercise.starter_code:
def build_dom():
    # Browser uses a stack to build the tree
    # This is essentially a __________ traversal
    method = "Depth-First Search"
    return method
hints: The browser goes deep into a tag's children before moving to the next sibling
